metric_definition: Number of active Gaussian primitives loaded or rendered for a scene or frame.
collection_method: Read checkpoint metadata, renderer statistics, or paper artifact logs.
required_hardware_or_tool: 3DGS codebase; renderer logs; gsplat or Nerfstudio statistics
interpretation_notes: Quality, memory and frame time can all change with pruning or densification; record scene and quality target.

metric_definition: Elapsed time from camera input to rendered RGB frame.
collection_method: Renderer timer, GPU event timing, Nsight timeline, or paper-reported frame time.
required_hardware_or_tool: GPU runtime profiler; FPGA board timer; ASIC simulator or chip measurement
interpretation_notes: FPS hides tail and resolution differences; record resolution, scene, quality and warmup policy.
notes: Use with PF-3D-005 for quality-normalized comparisons.

metric_definition: Time split across culling, projection, binning, sorting and alpha blending.
collection_method: Instrument renderer stages or use timeline/profiler regions.
required_hardware_or_tool: Renderer source instrumentation; Nsight Systems/Compute; FPGA trace counters
used_by_papers_or_benchmarks: GSCore; GCC; A-SSCC Gaussian Splats processor
interpretation_notes: Stage names are not standardized across renderers; align definitions before comparing papers.
notes: Record whether sort and blend are fused.

metric_definition: Distribution of Gaussians assigned to screen tiles or sorted per tile.
collection_method: Collect per-tile lists from renderer instrumentation or hardware counters.
required_hardware_or_tool: 3DGS rasterizer instrumentation; GPU profiler; FPGA custom counters
interpretation_notes: Averages hide hot tiles; record percentile or histogram, not only mean length.
notes: Some accelerator sources are title/abstract or preprint level.

metric_definition: Image quality of rendered views against held-out views.
collection_method: Run benchmark evaluation script or read paper table for the same dataset/split.
required_hardware_or_tool: Dataset ground truth; evaluation script; LPIPS implementation
interpretation_notes: Quality metrics are dataset- and split-sensitive; speedups without quality are incomplete.
notes: No evaluation script was run in this task.

metric_definition: Peak memory footprint for Gaussian parameters, gradients, optimizer state and renderer buffers.
collection_method: Profiler memory peak, framework memory allocator logs, or paper-reported memory table.
required_hardware_or_tool: CUDA memory profiler; PyTorch memory stats; system memory monitor
interpretation_notes: State size depends on densification/pruning schedule and precision; record training step or checkpoint.
notes: Recent large-scene systems need separate artifact checks.

metric_definition: Tail latency of online tracking, map update and render steps in Gaussian-map SLAM.
collection_method: Timestamp SLAM pipeline stages or use system profiler trace.
required_hardware_or_tool: SplaTAM or SLAM implementation; GPU/CPU profiler; robot/AR sequence logs
used_by_papers_or_benchmarks: SplaTAM; Gaussian Splatting SLAM; SGS-SLAM; GauSPU
interpretation_notes: Average FPS can miss control-loop failures; record per-stage tail latency and dropped frames.

metric_definition: Elapsed time from request arrival to first generated token.
collection_method: Serving benchmark client timestamp, engine logs or GenAI-Perf/LLMPerf output.
required_hardware_or_tool: LLM serving engine; synchronized client clock; benchmark harness
interpretation_notes: TTFT mixes queueing, tokenization, prefill and network effects; record load and prompt length.

metric_definition: Average or percentile inter-token decode time after first token.
collection_method: Serving benchmark timestamps or engine decode-step metrics.
required_hardware_or_tool: LLM server logs; benchmark harness; GPU profiler
interpretation_notes: Report prefill and decode separately; long output length can mask warmup and queue effects.

metric_definition: Generated tokens or completed requests served per unit time under a stated load.
collection_method: Load generator output; server counters; benchmark harness.
required_hardware_or_tool: LLM serving engine; benchmark client; MLPerf LoadGen
used_by_papers_or_benchmarks: vLLM benchmark; LLMPerf; GenAI-Perf; MLPerf Inference
interpretation_notes: Throughput without latency SLO can overstate useful capacity.
notes: Record prompt and output length distribution.

metric_definition: Throughput that satisfies a specified latency SLO.
unit: tokens/s within SLO; requests/s within SLO
collection_method: Benchmark client classifies requests by latency threshold.
required_hardware_or_tool: Serving benchmark harness; request trace
interpretation_notes: SLO definition must include TTFT, TPOT or end-to-end latency; do not compare different SLOs directly.

metric_definition: Peak memory consumed by KV cache and related allocator metadata.
collection_method: Engine memory counters, GPU memory profiler, or allocator page/block accounting.
required_hardware_or_tool: vLLM/PagedAttention logs; CUDA memory profiler; engine instrumentation
interpretation_notes: Peak cache depends on active sequence lengths, block size, quantization and prefix reuse.
notes: Record model layers, heads and KV precision.

metric_definition: Memory consumed per generated or stored token in the KV cache.
collection_method: Calculate from model architecture and KV precision or read engine accounting.
required_hardware_or_tool: Model config; serving engine allocator; profiler
used_by_papers_or_benchmarks: vLLM; KIVI; FlexGen; PACT CXL/KV rows
interpretation_notes: Architecture-derived bytes ignore fragmentation; measured bytes include allocator policy.
notes: Separate logical KV size from physical reserved memory.

metric_definition: Fraction of prompt tokens or requests served from reusable prefix/cache state.
collection_method: Serving runtime cache statistics or request-trace replay.
required_hardware_or_tool: SGLang/vLLM style prefix cache; workload trace
interpretation_notes: High hit rate can hide compute but increase memory pressure; record eviction policy.

metric_definition: Useful tokens produced per routing or draft-verification work unit in MoE/speculative decoding.
unit: tokens/second; acceptance rate; expert load balance
collection_method: Runtime counters for expert routing, draft acceptance and verification iterations.
required_hardware_or_tool: MoE/speculative runtime instrumentation; GPU profiler
used_by_papers_or_benchmarks: Switch Transformer; Mixtral; speculative decoding systems; TensorRT-LLM
interpretation_notes: A faster draft model is not enough if acceptance is low or routing is imbalanced.
notes: Keep separate from dense decode throughput.

metric_definition: Energy consumed per generated token under a stated load and measurement boundary.
collection_method: Power meter integration, GPU/NPU telemetry, or board-level measurement divided by token count.
required_hardware_or_tool: External power meter; NVML; board telemetry; datacenter power logs
used_by_papers_or_benchmarks: MLPerf Power; LLM serving papers; edge LLM rows
interpretation_notes: Telemetry scope must be explicit: chip, board, node or wall power.

metric_definition: Input sensor frequency and maximum allowed processing latency for closed-loop operation.
collection_method: Read dataset/robot timestamps and application deadline configuration.
required_hardware_or_tool: Robot middleware logs; ROS 2 traces; dataset timestamps
interpretation_notes: A benchmark can be accurate but unusable if it misses the sensor deadline.

metric_definition: Latency of front-end tracking or pose-estimation update per sensor frame.
collection_method: Instrument SLAM stage timestamps or use tracing middleware.
required_hardware_or_tool: SLAM implementation; ROS 2 trace; CPU/GPU profiler
interpretation_notes: Tail latency and relocalization cases should not be collapsed into a mean.

metric_definition: Time to update or query map structures such as voxel, octree, ESDF or collision maps.
collection_method: Instrument mapping/planning code around update and query calls.
required_hardware_or_tool: OctoMap; Voxblox; OMPL; profiler traces
interpretation_notes: Input distribution matters: free-space updates, occupied voxels and collision checks differ.
notes: Record map size and query/update mix.

metric_definition: Trajectory accuracy against ground truth or reference trajectory.
collection_method: Dataset evaluation script or benchmark scoring tool.
required_hardware_or_tool: EuRoC; KITTI; TUM RGB-D; SLAM evaluation scripts
interpretation_notes: Accuracy can trade off against latency and power; compare only on the same sequence and settings.

metric_definition: Time from observation availability to action output for VLA or learned policy inference.
collection_method: Timestamp camera/state input and action output in inference loop.
required_hardware_or_tool: Robot policy runtime; GPU/NPU profiler; ROS 2 trace
used_by_papers_or_benchmarks: RT-1; RT-2; OpenVLA; OpenVLA-OFT; Diffusion Policy; ACT
interpretation_notes: Batch=1 and p99 latency matter more than cloud batch throughput for robot control.

metric_definition: Task completion or policy quality on a named benchmark or robot task.
collection_method: Benchmark evaluator, simulator, or robot rollout logs.
required_hardware_or_tool: LIBERO; Open X-Embodiment task suite; robot evaluation setup
interpretation_notes: Offline dataset score is not the same as real robot success.

metric_definition: Power draw and thermal behavior during robot workload execution.
collection_method: Board telemetry, external power meter or robot power rail logging.
required_hardware_or_tool: Jetson/edge telemetry; power meter; SoC monitor
used_by_papers_or_benchmarks: RobotPerf; edge VLA papers; embedded SLAM accelerators
interpretation_notes: Power must include the same scope across systems; thermal throttling changes latency.

metric_definition: Sustained dense linear-algebra or tensor-operation rate.
collection_method: Benchmark output, library profiler or hardware performance counter.
required_hardware_or_tool: HPL; BLAS/cuBLAS/CUTLASS; MLPerf LoadGen; GPU profiler
interpretation_notes: Dense FLOP/s is not comparable to sparse or graph workloads without workload context.

metric_definition: Useful sparse/graph work completed per second.
collection_method: HPCG, Graph500, GAPBS or kernel-specific output.
required_hardware_or_tool: HPCG; Graph500; GAPBS; sparse-kernel profiler
interpretation_notes: Sparse FLOP/s can be misleading; report graph/matrix statistics and memory traffic.
notes: Separate BFS, SSSP, SpMV and PageRank.

metric_definition: Operations divided by data movement at a named memory level.
collection_method: Roofline analysis from measured counters or analytic traffic model.
required_hardware_or_tool: Profiler counters; Roofline tool; manual operation/byte model
used_by_papers_or_benchmarks: Roofline method; Nsight Compute Roofline; HPC reports
interpretation_notes: The memory level must be explicit; DRAM bytes and cache bytes give different values.
notes: Use as bottleneck classifier, not final proof alone.

metric_definition: Measured data movement or effective memory bandwidth for a kernel/application.
collection_method: Hardware counters, profiler memory metrics, or analytic model.
required_hardware_or_tool: Nsight Compute; perf; ROCProfiler; FPGA memory counters
interpretation_notes: Effective bandwidth depends on cache policy, compression and input layout.
notes: Record whether read, write and cache traffic are separated.

metric_definition: Time spent in communication, collectives, synchronization or global reductions.
collection_method: Timeline profiler, MPI instrumentation or benchmark phase timers.
required_hardware_or_tool: Nsight Systems; MPI profiler; NAS/HPCG phase output
interpretation_notes: Overlap can hide communication; state whether overlapped time is included.

metric_definition: Elapsed time for one kernel, benchmark phase or full benchmark invocation.
collection_method: Benchmark timer, trace range, or profiler event timing.
required_hardware_or_tool: PolyBench; Rodinia; Parboil; Nsight Systems; perf
interpretation_notes: Short kernels need repeated runs and warmup; report compiler flags and input size.
notes: Do not mix host setup time with kernel-only time unless stated.

metric_definition: Efficiency relative to ideal speedup as nodes, devices or cores increase.
collection_method: Compute from benchmark throughput or runtime at multiple scales.
required_hardware_or_tool: HPL/HPCG/MLPerf logs; cluster scheduler records; profiler
used_by_papers_or_benchmarks: HPL; HPCG; MLPerf; SC venue rows
interpretation_notes: Requires same problem scaling policy; weak and strong scaling differ.
notes: Record node count, interconnect and scaling mode.

metric_definition: FPGA resource consumption after HLS, synthesis or implementation.
unit: LUT; FF; BRAM; URAM; DSP count and percent
collection_method: Read HLS, synthesis or implementation reports.
required_hardware_or_tool: Vitis HLS; Vivado; Intel Quartus; vendor report parser
used_by_papers_or_benchmarks: Vitis HLS papers; Rosetta; HeteroCL; TAPA/AutoSA rows
interpretation_notes: Resource percent is not runtime utilization; post-implementation is more reliable than HLS estimate.

metric_definition: Achieved or estimated clock frequency and timing margin.
collection_method: Read synthesis/implementation timing reports or board clock configuration.
required_hardware_or_tool: Vitis/Vivado timing reports; board build logs
interpretation_notes: A high HLS-estimated clock can fail during implementation; record timing-closed status.
notes: Use negative slack as failed timing closure.

metric_definition: Cycles between starting consecutive loop or function iterations in a pipeline.
collection_method: Read HLS synthesis report or dataflow viewer.
required_hardware_or_tool: Vitis HLS; HLS report parser
used_by_papers_or_benchmarks: Rosetta; AutoSA; TAPA; HeteroCL; Vitis HLS reports
interpretation_notes: II can be invalidated by memory stalls and FIFO backpressure on board.

metric_definition: Estimated or measured latency for an HLS function, loop or accelerator kernel.
collection_method: HLS report, RTL co-simulation, board counter or host timer.
required_hardware_or_tool: Vitis HLS; Vivado; board runtime; RealProbe
interpretation_notes: Tripcount, fill/drain cycles and host transfer inclusion must be explicit.
notes: Mark estimate, co-sim or board measurement.

metric_definition: Measured bandwidth to HBM, DDR, PCIe or host memory.
collection_method: Memory microbenchmark, Vitis Analyzer transfer profile, or board counters.
required_hardware_or_tool: Shuhai; Vitis Analyzer; board memory controller counters
used_by_papers_or_benchmarks: Shuhai; HBM FPGA papers; FPGA host/cloud rows
interpretation_notes: Access pattern, burst length and bank/channel mapping can dominate results.

metric_definition: Cycles or time where dataflow stages are stalled, starved or backpressured.
collection_method: Dataflow viewer, FIFO occupancy trace, on-FPGA profiler or simulator trace.
required_hardware_or_tool: Vitis HLS dataflow viewer; RealProbe; LightningSim
interpretation_notes: Do not infer pipeline balance from II alone; classify stall cause.

metric_definition: Measured FPGA board/chip power and energy per operation or frame.
collection_method: Board telemetry, external power meter or vendor power analysis.
required_hardware_or_tool: On-board sensors; Vivado power; external meter
interpretation_notes: Vendor power estimates and wall power are not interchangeable.

metric_definition: Access latency for local DRAM, CXL memory, pooled memory or remote memory.
collection_method: Pointer-chase microbenchmark, memory benchmark, simulator or hardware counter.
required_hardware_or_tool: CXL platform; far-memory prototype; simulator; perf counters
used_by_papers_or_benchmarks: Clio; Nimble; SDFM; CXL venue rows
interpretation_notes: Latency distribution and page/cache hit state matter; single average is weak evidence.

metric_definition: Sustained bandwidth to CXL, pooled, remote or far memory under a stated access pattern.
collection_method: STREAM-style benchmark, memory benchmark, simulator, or application counter.
required_hardware_or_tool: CXL memory expander; remote-memory testbed; simulator
used_by_papers_or_benchmarks: Clio; far-memory systems; CXL memory rows
interpretation_notes: Bandwidth depends on read/write mix, queue depth and contention.

metric_definition: Number of pages or bytes migrated between memory tiers.
collection_method: OS/runtime counters, trace instrumentation, or simulator logs.
required_hardware_or_tool: Tiered-memory runtime; CXL/far-memory prototype; simulator
used_by_papers_or_benchmarks: Nimble; SDFM; tiered-memory systems; LLM KV offload rows
interpretation_notes: Migration can improve hit rate while adding traffic; record trigger policy.

metric_definition: Fraction of memory-array, bank or near-memory compute resources doing useful work.
collection_method: Simulator counters, hardware performance counters, or paper-reported utilization.
required_hardware_or_tool: PIM/CIM simulator; chip measurement setup; architecture model
interpretation_notes: High theoretical bandwidth does not imply high array utilization.
notes: Definitions differ across analog, digital and DRAM-PIM work.

metric_definition: Cost and accuracy impact of ADC/DAC, quantization, analog variation or approximate compute.
collection_method: Circuit/architecture simulator, chip measurement, or evaluation table.
required_hardware_or_tool: CIM/PIM simulator; circuit model; accuracy evaluation script
used_by_papers_or_benchmarks: ReRAM Transformer rows; CIMFormer; LoRA-CIM; analog-PIM papers
interpretation_notes: Analog and quantized systems need both system and accuracy metrics.
notes: Use conservative status unless circuit artifacts are checked.

metric_definition: Energy cost per memory access, operation or LLM token for PIM/CIM/offload design.
collection_method: Simulator energy model, chip measurement, or power integration.
required_hardware_or_tool: Accelergy/Timeloop; circuit simulator; silicon measurement; power meter
interpretation_notes: Energy accounting must state whether peripheral, host and data movement are included.

metric_definition: Fraction of work, memory accesses or bytes handled near memory rather than on the host.
collection_method: Simulator counters, trace classification, or runtime instrumentation.
required_hardware_or_tool: NDP/PIM simulator; graph benchmark trace; host runtime logs
used_by_papers_or_benchmarks: GraphSAR; connection-based PIM; linked-list traversal rows
interpretation_notes: High offload ratio can still lose if synchronization and reductions dominate.
notes: Candidate field for future artifact checks.

metric_definition: Profiler, compiler, driver, benchmark and library versions used for measurement.
collection_method: Record from command output, report metadata or artifact README.
required_hardware_or_tool: Profiler CLI; package manager; benchmark harness
used_by_papers_or_benchmarks: Nsight; ROCProfiler; Vitis; MLPerf; project run cards
interpretation_notes: Different versions change counters, kernels and rules; always capture exact version.
notes: For this task, versions were not collected because no benchmarks ran.

metric_definition: Exact input sizes, scene, graph, sequence length, model, benchmark class or dataset split.
collection_method: Read benchmark configuration, dataset manifest, artifact script or paper table.
required_hardware_or_tool: Benchmark harness; dataset files; paper appendix
used_by_papers_or_benchmarks: All workload families in this inventory
interpretation_notes: Most accelerator claims are not comparable without input shape and dataset.

metric_definition: Identity and configuration of the baseline used for speedup, efficiency or energy comparison.
collection_method: Extract from artifact README, scripts, paper table or local run log.
required_hardware_or_tool: Repository metadata; build scripts; run card
used_by_papers_or_benchmarks: All workload families in this inventory
interpretation_notes: Speedup without baseline provenance is weak evidence.
notes: This inventory does not claim speedups.

metric_definition: Number of runs and observed variability for reported metrics.
collection_method: Benchmark harness statistics or repeated measurement log.
required_hardware_or_tool: Benchmark runner; statistical script
used_by_papers_or_benchmarks: MLPerf; HPC benchmarks; artifact evaluation reports
interpretation_notes: Single-run measurements are fragile for short kernels or loaded systems.
notes: Not applicable to this no-run task except as future requirement.

metric_definition: Hardware scope included in power or energy measurement.
collection_method: Read power tool configuration, meter wiring notes or paper methods.
required_hardware_or_tool: NVML; RAPL/powercap; board sensors; external meter; MLPerf Power
used_by_papers_or_benchmarks: MLPerf Power; FPGA/ASIC papers; edge robotics rows
interpretation_notes: Energy numbers are not comparable when boundaries differ.

metric_definition: Evidence level for whether code, data, scripts and hardware are public and runnable.
collection_method: URL inspection, README extraction, artifact badge check and run-card update.
required_hardware_or_tool: Browser/source check; repository metadata; project source policy
used_by_papers_or_benchmarks: ACM artifact badging; MLCommons; venue artifact rows
interpretation_notes: Public repository does not imply local execution; hardware-specific artifacts may remain access-restricted.
notes: Use statuses such as runnable_unchecked and access_restricted; do not write reproduced.
